reviewer_risk: family name is baseline/teacher/reference, not target lineage
example_title: SLM as Guardian: Pioneering AI Safety with Small Language Models
expert_decision: Use only as upstream-dependence evidence, not as a target-family claim.
why_boundary_matters: Teacher, parent, and baseline mentions are useful for reuse measurement but can be wrong as target-family labels.
metadata_fix: Use typed relation/evidence fields rather than a generic model or release link.

reviewer_risk: multi-family mention cannot be forced into one lineage
example_title: ixi-GEN: Efficient Industrial sLLMs through Domain Adaptive Continual Pretraining
expert_decision: Use as a family-attention signal but not as a unique primary lineage.
why_boundary_matters: A paper can study several backbones or targets; forcing one lineage would overstate precision.
metadata_fix: Use typed relation/evidence fields rather than a generic model or release link.

reviewer_risk: generic bucket can hide named family evidence
example_title: Large Language Models are Superpositions of All Characters: Attaining Arbitrary Role-play via Self-Alignment
expert_decision: Treat as a false negative for family coverage.
why_boundary_matters: Generic/no-family buckets sometimes hide named target families and should be counted as uncertainty.
metadata_fix: Use typed relation/evidence fields rather than a generic model or release link.

reviewer_risk: wrong family label can create false provenance
example_title: PILLOW : Enhancing Efficient Instruction Fine-tuning via Prompt Matching
expert_decision: Exclude from strict lineage claims and use only in calibrated aggregate analysis.
why_boundary_matters: This is the failure mode that motivates evidence-tiered family sensitivity.
metadata_fix: Use typed relation/evidence fields rather than a generic model or release link.

example_title: base vs merge/mixed or packaging overlay
evidence_excerpt: A single repository can expose base-model, merge, quantization, or deployment signals at the same time.
why_boundary_matters: A single repository can expose base-model, merge, quantization, or deployment signals at the same time.
metadata_fix: Store primary artifact role separately from packaging overlays.

example_title: family label names an upstream parent but not the target artifact
evidence_excerpt: Family labels help discovery but can mislead if treated as exact genealogy.
why_boundary_matters: Family labels help discovery but can mislead if treated as exact genealogy.
metadata_fix: Use typed upstream relations such as base, teacher, adapter target, merge input, quantization source, and baseline.

example_title: paper names GPT-4 or another teacher/reference model
evidence_excerpt: Teacher or evaluator mentions can be mistaken for the artifact's own family.
why_boundary_matters: Teacher or evaluator mentions can be mistaken for the artifact's own family.
metadata_fix: Separate target artifact, teacher model, evaluator, and baseline fields.

example_title: family term appears in context but does not identify the artifact lineage
evidence_excerpt: Text mentions alone overstate provenance precision.
why_boundary_matters: Text mentions alone overstate provenance precision.
metadata_fix: Attach provenance confidence and evidence type to family labels.

example_title: paper states availability but lacks explicit code, weights, data, or recipe evidence
evidence_excerpt: Binary open/not-open metadata hides large differences in reuse and preservation readiness.
why_boundary_matters: Binary open/not-open metadata hides large differences in reuse and preservation readiness.
metadata_fix: Expose separate release fields for code, weights/adapters, data/evaluation assets, scripts, and complete recipes.

example_title: paper and repository records exist but are not explicitly connected
evidence_excerpt: Curators cannot reliably connect scholarly claims to artifact records.
why_boundary_matters: Curators cannot reliably connect scholarly claims to artifact records.
metadata_fix: Add DOI/arXiv-to-repository and repository-to-paper relation fields.
